elements: Full-viewport layered wave backgrounds with parallax depth
Dark-to-light gradient transitions simulating ocean depth zones
SVG path animations for flowing connection lines between content
Horizontal scroll-triggered content reveals with current-like easing
howWeAreDifferent: Our waves are functional section dividers, not decorative — they separate semantic content regions
Navigation items physically bob with GSAP sine animation rather than static positioned links
Color palette maps to a specific depth metaphor (surface foam white through deep navy)
Every interactive element produces ripple feedback, creating a tactile water-surface feel

elements: Clean stat card grid with animated counters
Smooth view transitions between dashboard sections
Minimal dark surfaces with focused accent color usage
Activity feed with chronological current-like flow
howWeAreDifferent: Stats physically rise from below the viewport (seafloor metaphor) rather than fading in place
Chart line is a literal waveform drawn with GSAP stroke animation, not a standard chart library
Activity items slide in horizontally like a flowing river current
Surface cards float with continuous idle bobbing animation

inspirationReferences.source: National Geographic Ocean Portal — Depth Layer Storytelling
elements: Content organized by depth zones (surface, twilight, midnight, abyss)
Bioluminescent accent effects on deep-sea organism imagery
Vertical scroll driving descent through ocean layers
Sonar and submarine control panel aesthetic for data displays
howWeAreDifferent: Depth-zone organization is applied to a project management tool (surface=overview, mid=tasks, deep=notes)
Bioluminescence is used for entity relationship indicators in the schema planner, not decoration
Submarine control panel metaphor drives actual settings UI (gauge SVGs for metrics, valve toggles for booleans)
The sonar pulse concept powers the AI chat interface — messages arrive as depth charges from different layers

uniqueDesignElements.element: Horizontal Stream Navigation with Sine-Wave Bob
uniqueDesignElements.description: Top-fixed nav bar where each item animates with staggered vertical displacement using GSAP sine easing, creating the appearance of items floating on a water surface. Click produces an expanding ripple from the touch point.

View switching animates outgoing content to the left (carried away by the current) and incoming content from the right (merging current). This creates a continuous directional flow metaphor rather than standard fade transitions.

The project workspace organizes information into surface (overview), mid-depth (active tasks), and deep (notes/context) layers, separated by animated SVG wave dividers. Each layer has distinct visual weight and border accent.
Project cards styled as vessels with SVG wave hulls, mast details, and continuous GSAP yoyo bobbing animation. Each card bobs at a slightly different frequency to prevent synchronized motion.

Entity relationship lines in the schema planner use SVG paths with a gradient that pulses between accent colors, simulating deep-sea bioluminescent organisms. Entity nodes have glowing indicator dots.
AI chat messages arrive from the deep (bottom) with a pulsing indicator dot, while user messages surface upward. Each message has a sonar pulse animation on its status indicator.

Settings page uses circular SVG gauge meters for system metrics, toggle switches styled as submarine valves, and range sliders labeled as depth controls. All gauges animate their fill on view entrance.

Ideas are displayed as bubble cards that float upward with idle animation. The input field sits at the bottom (ocean floor), and new ideas conceptually rise from it. Bubbles have a shimmer highlight that sweeps across them.
Directory tree structure uses coral branch metaphor with connected vertical lines styled as coral stems. Files at branch tips are styled as reef creatures with size annotations.
Every major content section boundary uses unique SVG wave path dividers rather than horizontal lines. Dividers have subtle horizontal drift animation powered by GSAP.
